Examples.Title: Delete file
Examples.Description: Permanently delete a file from the system. This action cannot be undone.
Examples.Code:
### Delete file
# @name gatewayDeleteFile
DELETE {{host}}/v1/files/{{file_id}}
Authorization: Bearer {{token}}
Accept: application/json

## Responses
# 200
# Content-Type: application/json

Examples.Title: Download file
Examples.Description: Download the binary content of a file. The response will contain the file data in the appropriate MIME type.
Examples.Code:
### Download file
# @name gatewayDownloadFile
GET {{host}}/v1/files/{{file_id}}/download
Authorization: Bearer {{token}}
Accept: application/octet-stream

## Responses
# 200
# Description: File download successful
# Content-Type: application/octet-stream

Examples.Title: Retrieve file
Examples.Description: Retrieve detailed information about a specific file, including its metadata, status, and associated graphs.
Examples.Code:
### Retrieve file
# @name gatewayGetFile
GET {{host}}/v1/files/{{file_id}}
Authorization: Bearer {{token}}
Accept: application/json

## Responses
# 200
# Content-Type: application/json

Examples.Title: List files
Examples.Description: Retrieve a paginated list of files with optional filtering by status, graph association, and file type.
Examples.Setup: This example assumes `using Writer;` is in scope and `apiKey` contains the required credential.

Examples.Title: Retry failed files
Examples.Description: Retry processing of files that previously failed to process. This will re-attempt the processing of the specified files.
Examples.Code:
### Retry failed files
# @name gatewayRetryFailedFiles
POST {{host}}/v1/files/retry
Authorization: Bearer {{token}}
Content-Type: application/json
Accept: application/json

{
  "file_ids": [
    "00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000"
  ]
}

## Responses
# 200
# Description: The retry request is being processed
# Content-Type: application/json

Examples.Title: Text generation
Examples.Description: Generate text completions using the specified model and prompt. This endpoint is useful for text generation tasks that don't require conversational context.
Examples.Setup: This example assumes `using Writer;` is in scope and `apiKey` contains the required credential.

Examples.Title: Generate from application
Examples.Description: Generate content from an existing no-code agent (formerly called no-code applications) with inputs.

Examples.Title: List models
Examples.Description: Retrieve a list of available models that can be used for text generation, chat completions, and other AI tasks.
Examples.Setup: This example assumes `using Writer;` is in scope and `apiKey` contains the required credential.

Examples.Title: Delete graph
Examples.Code:
### Delete graph
# @name deleteGraph
DELETE {{host}}/v1/graphs/{{graph_id}}
Authorization: Bearer {{token}}
Accept: application/json

## Responses
# 200
# Content-Type: application/json

Examples.Title: Question
Examples.Description: Ask a question to specified Knowledge Graphs.
Examples.Code:
### Question
# @name question
POST {{host}}/v1/graphs/question
Authorization: Bearer {{token}}
Content-Type: application/json
Accept: application/json, text/event-stream

{
  "graph_ids": [
    "00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000"
  ],
  "subqueries": false,
  "question": "string",
  "stream": false,
  "query_config": {
    "max_subquestions": 6,
    "search_weight": 50,
    "grounding_level": 0,
    "max_snippets": 30,
    "max_tokens": 4000,
    "keyword_threshold": 0.7,
    "semantic_threshold": 0.7,
    "inline_citations": false
  }
}

## Responses
# 200
# Content-Type: application/json, text/event-stream

Examples.Title: Remove file from graph
Examples.Description: Remove a file from a Knowledge Graph.
Examples.Code:
### Remove file from graph
# @name removeFileFromGraph
DELETE {{host}}/v1/graphs/{{graph_id}}/file/{{file_id}}
Authorization: Bearer {{token}}
Accept: application/json

## Responses
# 200
# Content-Type: application/json

Examples.Title: Text-to-graph
Examples.Description: Performs name entity recognition on the supplied text accepting a maximum of 35000 words.
Examples.Code:
### Text-to-graph
POST {{host}}/v1/tools/text-to-graph
Authorization: Bearer {{token}}
Content-Type: application/json
Accept: application/json

{
  "text": "string"
}

## Responses
# 200
# Content-Type: application/json

Examples.Title: Web search
Examples.Description: Search the web for information about a given query and return relevant results with source URLs.
Examples.Setup: This example assumes `using Writer;` is in scope and `apiKey` contains the required credential.

Examples.Title: Translate text
Examples.Description: Translate text from one language to another.
Examples.Setup: This example assumes `using Writer;` is in scope and `apiKey` contains the required credential.

Examples.Title: Analyze images
Examples.Description: Submit images and documents with a prompt to generate an analysis. Supports JPG, PNG, PDF, and TXT files up to 7MB each.
Examples.Setup: This example assumes `using Writer;` is in scope and `apiKey` contains the required credential.
